action.reasoning: Observation: Model stuck at ~17% val_acc (broken metric) but eval showed 53% overall after 3 epochs. NaN losses throughout training. Analysis: bfloat16 doesn't need GradScaler - using scaler with bfloat16 causes NaN. Mixup also adds instability. The val_acc in training is wrong (eval shows 53% but training shows 17%). Need to completely fix training: remove scaler, remove mixup, fix val computation. Decision: Rewrite train.py properly - no scaler for bfloat16, no mixup, proper LR schedule, resume from best checkpoint. Also add class-balanced sampling to improve worst-region accuracy.
